displayTitle: Coherent vs. Incoherent: How a Fiberscope Carries an Image

beats.narration: Fiberscopes deliver a picture around corners — through thousands of hair-fine glass fibers. The trick is how those fibers are arranged.

payload.title: THE FIBERSCOPE
payload.sub: How thousands of glass fibers carry an image around a bend.

beats.narration: Each fiber traps light by total internal reflection — bouncing off the core-cladding boundary around every bend. Each fiber carries one dot of the picture.
A coherent bundle keeps every fiber in the same position at both ends, so dots reassemble into the picture. Incoherent bundles are jumbled — light only.
Snap a fiber, and its dot goes black forever. So remember: coherent bundles carry the picture, incoherent bundles carry the light.
